perturbation.original_text: This Non-Disclosure Agreement (the "Agreement") is made and entered into as of the later of the two signature dates below by and between (CHINESE CHARACTERS) Microsoft (China) Co., Ltd., LTD, a PRC corporation ("Microsoft"), and (CHINESE CHARACTERS) ATA Testing Authority, Beijing Co., Ltd. ("ATA"), a PRC corporation ("Company").
perturbation.changed_text: This Non-Disclosure Agreement (the "Agreement") is made and entered into as of the later of the two signature dates below by and between (CHINESE CHARACTERS) Microsoft (China) Co., Ltd., LTD, a PRC corporation ("Microsoft"), and (CHINESE CHARACTERS) ATA Testing Authority, Beijing Co., Ltd. ("ATA"), a PRC corporation ("Vendor").
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Company" to "Vendor" in the opening paragraph creates an in-text contradiction. Throughout the document, "Company" is used. Now, the introductory definition conflicts with the subsequent usage, leading to confusion about which entity is bound by the obligations ascribed to the 'Company'. This also creates ambiguity on which entity (ATA or Vendor) should be considered to have these obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (c) The undersigned Receiving Party may disclose Confidential Information only to Receiving Party's employees and consultants on a need-to-know basis. The undersigned Receiving Party will have executed or shall execute appropriate written agreements with its employees and consultants sufficient to enable Receiving Party to enforce all the provisions of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Company' with 'Vendor' in the definition of ATA. The contradicted text refers to obligations of the 'Company'. This change creates a direct contradiction, because it is not clear whether 'Vendor' is also bound by the obligations described in the contradicted text, which specifically refers to 'Company'. This creates ambiguity and uncertainty within the document, meaning there is a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Confidential Information shall not include any information, however designated, that: (i) is or subsequently becomes publicly available without Receiving Party's breach of any obligation owed Disclosing Party; (ii) became known to Receiving Party prior to Disclosing Party's disclosure of such information to Receiving Party pursuant to the terms of this Agreement; (iii) became known to Receiving Party from a source other than Disclosing Party other than by the breach of an obligation of confidentiality owed to Disclosing Party; (iv) is independently developed by Receiving Party; or (v) constitutes Feedback (as defined in Section 5 of this Agreement).
perturbation.changed_text: Protected Information shall not include any information, however designated, that: (i) is or subsequently becomes publicly available without Receiving Party's breach of any obligation owed Disclosing Party; (ii) became known to Receiving Party prior to Disclosing Party's disclosure of such information to Receiving Party pursuant to the terms of this Agreement; (iii) became known to Receiving Party from a source other than Disclosing Party other than by the breach of an obligation of confidentiality owed to Disclosing Party; (iv) is independently developed by Receiving Party; or (v) constitutes Feedback (as defined in Section 5 of this Agreement).
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Confidential Information" to "Protected Information" creates an in-text contradiction. Throughout the document, it refers to "Confidential Information", which implies that Protected Information is not Confidential Information. This change leads to uncertainty about which information is actually protected under the agreement. This undermines the entire purpose of the NDA. Furthermore, the definition in 1(a) would not apply to Protected Information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (a) Receiving Party shall: (i) Refrain from disclosing any Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party to third parties for five (5) years following the date that Disclosing Party first discloses such Confidential Information to Receiving Party, except as expressly provided in Sections 2(b) and 2(c) of this Agreement; (ii) Take reasonable security precautions, at least as great as the precautions it takes to protect its own confidential information, but no less than reasonable care, to keep confidential the Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party; (iii) Refrain from disclosing, reproducing, summarizing and/or distributing Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party except in pursuance of Receiving Party's business relationship with Disclosing Party, and only as otherwise provided hereunder; and (iv) Refrain from reverse engineering, decompiling or disassembling any software code and/or pre-release hardware devices disclosed by Disclosing Party to Receiving Party under the terms of this Agreement, except as expressly permitted by applicable law.
perturbation.justification: The change from "Confidential Information" to "Protected Information" creates a contradiction. The contradicted text refers specifically to obligations regarding "Confidential Information". By changing the definition, the altered text implies that "Protected Information" is distinct from "Confidential Information", thus the obligations in the contradicted section do not apply to "Protected Information", creating a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: (a) Receiving Party shall: (i) Refrain from disclosing any Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party to third parties for five (5) years following the date that Disclosing Party first discloses such Confidential Information to Receiving Party, except as expressly provided in Sections 2(b) and 2(c) of this Agreement; (ii) Take reasonable security precautions, at least as great as the precautions it takes to protect its own confidential information, but no less than reasonable care, to keep confidential the Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party; (iii) Refrain from disclosing, reproducing, summarizing and/or distributing Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party except in pursuance of Receiving Party's business relationship with Disclosing Party, and only as otherwise provided hereunder; and (iv) Refrain from reverse engineering, decompiling or disassembling any software code and/or pre-release hardware devices disclosed by Disclosing Party to Receiving Party under the terms of this Agreement, except as expressly permitted by applicable law.
perturbation.changed_text: (a) Receiving Party shall: (i) Refrain from disclosing any Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party to third parties for ten (10) years following the date that Disclosing Party first discloses such Confidential Information to Receiving Party, except as expressly provided in Sections 2(b) and 2(c) of this Agreement; (ii) Take commercially reasonable security precautions to keep confidential the Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party; (iii) Refrain from disclosing, reproducing, summarizing and/or distributing Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party except in pursuance of Receiving Party's business relationship with Disclosing Party, and only as otherwise provided hereunder; and (iv) Refrain from reverse engineering, decompiling or disassembling any software code and/or pre-release hardware devices disclosed by Disclosing Party to Receiving Party under the terms of this Agreement, except as expressly permitted by applicable law.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the term from "reasonable security precautions, at least as great as the precautions it takes to protect its own confidential information, but no less than reasonable care" to "commercially reasonable security precautions" and from "five (5) years" to "ten (10) years", introduces an in-text contradiction regarding the duration and required effort to safeguard the Confidential Information. The original text indicates a more specific and perhaps a greater standard of care than just commercially reasonable. The new changes create uncertainty in the level of protection needed and the duration of the non-disclosure, potentially leading to legal disputes over whether the Receiving Party met their obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (a) "Confidential Information" means nonpublic information that a party to this Agreement ("Disclosing Party") designates as being confidential to the party that receives such information ("Receiving Party") or which, under the circumstances surrounding disclosure ought to be treated as confidential by the Receiving Party. "Confidential Information" includes, without limitation, information in tangible or intangible form relating to and/or including released or unreleased Disclosing Party software or hardware products, the marketing or promotion of any Disclosing Party product, Disclosing Party's business policies or practices, and information received from others that Disclosing Party is obligated to treat as confidential. Except as otherwise indicated in this Agreement, the term "Disclosing Party" also includes all Affiliates of the Disclosing Party and, except as otherwise indicated, the term "Receiving Party" also includes all Affiliates of the Receiving Party. An "Affiliate" means any person, partnership, joint venture, corporation or other form of enterprise, domestic or foreign, including but not limited to subsidiaries, that directly or indirectly, control, are controlled by, or are under common control with a party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the Receiving Party's obligation regarding Confidential Information. Specifically, it changes 'reasonable security precautions, at least as great as the precautions it takes to protect its own confidential information, but no less than reasonable care' to 'commercially reasonable security precautions' and extends the non-disclosure period from 'five (5) years' to 'ten (10) years'. This contradicts the definition of Confidential Information by altering the level of care and duration of protection expected from the Receiving Party. While the Contradicted Text does not specifically state the duration or level of care needed, it implies that the Receiving Party should treat it as confidential under the circumstances. By changing the standard of care to 'commercially reasonable' and extending the period, the modified text introduces ambiguity and potentially lowers the standard of care previously understood in the original agreement, hence creating a contradiction regarding how confidential information ought to be treated, as the effort and time has changed.

perturbation.original_text: (e)Receiving Party shall, at Disclosing Party's request, return all originals, copies, reproductions and summaries of Confidential Information and all other tangible materials and devices provided to the Receiving Party as Confidential Information, or at Disclosing Party's option, certify destruction of the same.
perturbation.changed_text: (e)Receiving Party shall, at Disclosing Party's request, return all originals, copies, reproductions and summaries of Confidential Information and all other tangible materials and devices provided to the Receiving Party as Confidential Information, or at Disclosing Party's option, ensure irreversible deletion of the same.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "certify destruction" to "ensure irreversible deletion" creates ambiguity. 'Certify destruction' implies a formal process and proof, whereas 'ensure irreversible deletion' is vague. This weakens the Disclosing Party's ability to verify compliance, because irreversible deletion doesn't imply any form of proof or certificate. This creates an in-text contradiction as to what the Receiving Party actually has to provide.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (e)Receiving Party shall, at Disclosing Party's request, return all originals, copies, reproductions and summaries of Confidential Information and all other tangible materials and devices provided to the Receiving Party as Confidential Information, or at Disclosing Party's option, ensure irreversible deletion of the same.
perturbation.justification: The change from "certify destruction" to "ensure irreversible deletion" creates a contradiction. The original text implies a verifiable process of destruction, providing the Disclosing Party with assurance. The changed text introduces ambiguity by only requiring 'irreversible deletion' without any proof or certification. This directly contradicts the implicit expectation in the original clause that the Disclosing Party would receive some form of verifiable confirmation of destruction, thus causing an in-text contradiction as to how the confidential information can be destroyed.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof. It shall not be modified except by a written agreement dated subsequent to the date of this Agreement and signed by both parties.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement constitutes the full understanding between the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof. It shall not be modified except by mutual agreement of the parties.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "entire agreement" to "full understanding" and "written agreement dated subsequent to the date of this Agreement and signed by both parties" to "mutual agreement of the parties" introduces ambiguity regarding the formal requirements for modifications. "Full understanding" is less precise than "entire agreement", and "mutual agreement" lacks the strict requirement for a written and dated amendment, creating an in-text contradiction and potentially allowing for verbal or implied modifications, which contradicts the original intent for formal amendments. The original sentence also implies that both parties should sign the written agreement, while mutual agreement of the parties can be understood as something else.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement constitutes the full understanding between the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof. It shall not be modified except by mutual agreement of the parties. None of the provisions of this Agreement shall be deemed to have been waived by any act or acquiescence on the part of Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party, their agents, or employees, but only by an instrument in writing signed by an authorized employee of Disclosing Party and the Receiving Party. No waiver of any provision of this Agreement shall constitute a waiver of any other provision(s) or of the same provision on another occasion.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the requirements for modifying the agreement from a 'written agreement dated subsequent to the date of this Agreement and signed by both parties' to a less strict 'mutual agreement of the parties'. This introduces ambiguity, as 'mutual agreement' could be interpreted as verbal or implied, directly contradicting the language in the 'Contradicted Text' which requires 'an instrument in writing signed by an authorized employee of Disclosing Party and the Receiving Party' for waiving provisions. The change introduces conflicting modification and waiver requirements within the same document, leading to a strong in-text contradiction as defined by 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: If either Disclosing Party or the Receiving Party employs attorneys to enforce any rights arising out of or relating to this Agreement, the prevailing party shall be entitled to recover reasonable attorneys' fees and costs. This Agreement shall be construed and controlled by the laws of the People's Republic of China, and the parties further consent to submit any disputation arising from and/or in connection with this Agreement to China International Economic & Trade Arbitration Commission (CIETAC) for arbitration in Beijing in accordance with the Commission's arbitration rules in effect at the time of applying for arbitration. The arbitral award is final and binding upon both parties.
perturbation.changed_text: If either Disclosing Party or the Receiving Party retains legal counsel to enforce any rights arising out of or relating to this Agreement, each party shall bear their own legal expenses. This Agreement shall be interpreted in accordance with the principles of fairness and good faith, and the parties agree to resolve any disputes amicably through negotiation. Should negotiation fail, the parties may pursue mediation or other alternative dispute resolution methods. The decision reached through mediation shall be non-binding.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "employs attorneys to enforce any rights arising out of or relating to this Agreement, the prevailing party shall be entitled to recover reasonable attorneys' fees and costs" to "retains legal counsel to enforce any rights arising out of or relating to this Agreement, each party shall bear their own legal expenses" and the section regarding arbitration creates a direct contradiction in how disputes are resolved. The original section enforces the right to attorney fees for the prevailing party and a specific arbitration process, whereas the modified section prohibits attorney fees and imposes negotiation and mediation instead, with a non-binding decision. This creates uncertainty regarding which dispute resolution process applies and which legal standards should be followed, contradicting the legally binding nature of the original agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: If either Disclosing Party or the Receiving Party retains legal counsel to enforce any rights arising out of or relating to this Agreement, each party shall bear their own legal expenses. This Agreement shall be interpreted in accordance with the principles of fairness and good faith, and the parties agree to resolve any disputes amicably through negotiation. Should negotiation fail, the parties may pursue mediation or other alternative dispute resolution methods. The decision reached through mediation shall be non-binding.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the original text by altering the dispute resolution process, changing it from arbitration with attorney fees for the prevailing party to negotiation and mediation with each party bearing their own legal expenses and a non-binding decision. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text, meaning that it enforces the modified dispute resolution process. The original text enforced arbitration and attorney fees, whereas the modified text, which also happens to be the contradicted text, enforces negotiation and mediation with no attorney fees. This is a direct conflict, because the two sections are incompatible; you cannot both arbitrate and negotiate/mediate. It creates a strong in-text contradiction.
